mission.title: Install and Configure Composefs on Kubernetes
mission.description: Composefs provides a flexible mechanism to support read-only mountable filesystem trees, combining the reliability of disk images with the flexibility of files. Installing Composefs allows you to efficiently manage filesystem images while avoiding common drawbacks associated with traditional disk images.

steps.title: Add the Composefs Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Composefs Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add composefs https://charts.example.com/composefs
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Composefs using Helm
steps.description:
Install Composefs in a new namespace called 'composefs' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install composefs composefs/composefs --namespace composefs --create-namespace --version v1.0.8
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Composefs pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace composefs
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Composefs deployment to ensure it runs efficiently:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/composefs --namespace composefs --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Port forward to access Composefs
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Composefs service to access it:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/composefs --namespace composefs 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Composefs pods running in the 'composefs' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. Resource limits will be applied to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add composefs https://charts.example.com/composefs
helm repo update
helm install composefs composefs/composefs --namespace composefs --create-namespace --version v1.0.8

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
